segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dawn bled into the Deep Hall as the lichen shifted from bruise-blue to silver-green. With the light came the low sound -- felt first in teeth, then in boot-soles, then up through the ribs.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A breath-long pause -- did you imagine it? -- then:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ward-line held under Serana's palm -- chalk geometry and nine-stroke precision, the sigils she'd drawn before sleep still bright enough to matter. They stole some of the bass out of the air, damping the pulse like thick velvet over a drum. Not all of it. Not anymore.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Scrape from the forge corridor. Tap-tap at the sealed mine. A wet drag through floodwater that should have been still for centuries. The mountain answered with another dull knock that belonged to no mortal heart.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin woke to that slow count and the smell of old smoke and cold iron. The scale in his forearm held its coal-glow, the pulse tightened -- not warning, not ease -- like a hand at the small of his back urging him forward.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The familiar ache had settled in overnight to its hateful normal. He flexed carefully. He could manage small work today -- if he paid every time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana kept her hand on the runes. No prayer came. Nothing answered. The chalk held because chalk didn't need permission -- only ratios and repetition and the stubbornness of someone who hadn't stopped yet.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "They've ringed the entrances,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, voice low and steady.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "We don't wait to be tested to failure. We choose our ground."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex was already up, knives in hand, standing where the Illusion alcove threw tricky reflections into the corners.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Fast careful,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "And if that's impossible, just fast."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She glanced at Caelin's arm.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Beacon-boy, if you could stop being a lighthouse -- "

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Working on it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, and because the truth cost less than a lie, added,

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "I can't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik had grandfather's journal spread on the black table, brass goggles riding his brow, big fingers unshakably precise. His beard smelled faintly of beeswax oil and iron dust.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Forge Road's our best way out -- old ore route, still marked sound when Grandfather walked it. Six hours if stone loves us, twelve if it doesn't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen sat cross-legged near the Abjuration alcove where her vine-ink picked up a little of the ward-light, emerald threaded with silver-gold. She lay her palm to the floor and winced -- stone fever, pressure, the fabric of the place pulled too tight.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "This chamber holds for now,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Everything tied to the sanctum's bones is failing. Whatever hunts us is... patient. Learning the ward geometry."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A smear of darkness slid under the rune-line at her feet -- a thin filament of shadow testing the gap between two chiselled glyphs. The ward flared white-hot.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For one knife-bright instant the shadow became a living silhouette of its owner's hand -- tendons, knuckles, too many joints -- then ignited.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It fled screaming without a sound, scuttling back as a scatter of ash that ran like insects, burned to nothing before it reached the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one breathed.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "They're clever,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said softly from the Necromancy alcove, as if admiring a well-played move in someone else's game. Her purple eyes tracked the ward seam a beat longer.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Not clever enough."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: On the edge of the chamber Durgan stood with his back to stone, eyes pale and unblinking. His shadow lay neat at his boots. It twitched once toward the ward-line when the burned ash fled, and he crushed it flat until his jaw clicked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The pressure of holding it down built behind his eyes like fingers pressing into his temples. When he finally released his concentration, a thin warmth trickled from his left nostril. He wiped it with the back of his hand -- checking, confirming. The skin came away with a single red smear.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: First blood, he thought, and tucked his hand away before anyone could see. How many more before I can't stop it anymore?

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Move before they learn a new trick,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said. She pushed to her feet and didn't look back to check the wards. They held or they didn't. Either way, the only answer was forward.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex rolled a shoulder, felt the pull of healing ribs beneath.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Define intact."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Alive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Everything else is optional."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik snapped the journal shut and slung on his pack.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Forge Road starts through the transmutation arch. Watch your steps. The mountain is currently... structurally questionable but functional."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That phrase has never comforted me,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "It wasn't supposed to,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik replied cheerfully.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It's supposed to be true."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They packed to travel-light: water, rope, wedges and braces for Thornik, rations like stone, no spare comforts. The ward-line pulsed off-beat once, a tired strobe. The bass in the room thickened.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Go,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They went.
